_source: MIBiG BGC0000326 (isocomplestatin / complestatin) + Chiu HT et al. PNAS 2001;98(15):8548-53 PMID 11447274 'Molecular cloning and sequence analysis of the complestatin biosynthetic gene cluster'; PMC37473 full text; Park JW et al. ChemBioChem 2016;17(15):1442-7 PMID 27383040 (54.5 kb reconstituted cluster, heterologous expression in S. lividans TK24); fetched 2026-05-16
producer: Streptomyces lavendulae (S. chartreusis AN1542 strain also produces complestatin family per Park 2016)
compound_class: NRPS-derived cyclic glycopeptide-like; member of the vancomycin/teicoplanin/chloropeptin structural family
compound_target: C1q / C4b binding; classical-pathway C3 convertase inhibition; also HIV-1 gp120/CD4 inhibition (incidental)

module_substrates_predicted: module_1_loading: 3,5-dichloro-4-hydroxyphenylglycine (Cl2-Hpg) OR Hpg loaded then chlorinated

tailoring_enzymes.beta_hydroxylation_for_betaOHTyr: OxyD-type P450 (inferred; canonical glycopeptide biosynthesis pattern, not all annotated by Chiu 2001)

hpg_pathway.function: Tyr precursor → 4-hydroxyphenylpyruvate → 4-hydroxymandelate (HmaS) → 4-hydroxyphenylglyoxylate (Hmo) → L-Hpg (HpgT, uses Tyr as amino donor)
hpg_pathway.natively_supplied_in_Streptomyces: yes (encoded within the cluster; ambient tyrosine pool sufficient)

dichlorination.cofactor: alpha-ketoglutarate + Fe(II) + Cl- + O2 (canonical nonheme Fe halogenase)
dichlorination.host_cofactor_compatibility: Fe + alpha-KG ubiquitous; Cl- ubiquitous; molecular O2 required — incompatible with strict anaerobes

S_lividans_TK24.yield: production confirmed; sub-mg/L typical for NRPS megaclusters without optimization

E_coli.status: not reported for full complestatin cluster
E_coli.general_NRPS_jump_difficulty: high; requires Sfp-type PPTase coexpression, GC content mismatch (Streptomyces ~72% GC vs E. coli ~50% GC), oxygen-tolerant assembly OK

Bacteroides.status: not reported for any complestatin-class compound; no Bacteroides NRPS-megacluster heterologous expression precedent in the literature
Bacteroides.general_NRPS_jump_difficulty: very high; strict-to-facultative anaerobe, GC content mismatch (Streptomyces ~72% GC vs B. thetaiotaomicron ~43% GC), O2-dependent P450s and halogenase fundamentally incompatible with obligate anaerobe lifestyle

oxygen_dependence.implication: Without these tailoring steps, the linear peptide is produced but lacks the rigid crosslinked architecture required for high-affinity C1q/C4b binding. Complestatin without P450-mediated phenolic coupling is biologically inactive (Park 2016 demonstrated this with complestatin M55/S56 monocyclic deletion derivatives).
